text: Git branching is an essential part of version control, especially when working with a team. A common workflow involves creating feature branches for each new task or feature. This allows developers to work independently without affecting the main codebase. Once the work is complete, the changes need to be integrated through a process called merging. Before merging, the team can use pull requests to review the code, discuss improvements, and ensure quality. This structured workflow helps teams collaborate efficiently, track changes, and maintain a stable main branch for the project.
